title: Lateral Posture
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- Non-intubated unconscious patients.
- Syncope.
- Continuous vomiting or upper airway bleeding with decreased level of consciousness.
- Chest injuries (affected side down).

#### Procedure

Figure 1

- Select appropriate side on which to roll patient, taking into account injuries and location.

- Kneel at patient’s hips on opposite side.

- Remove belts and items from pockets that are likely to cause patient pain or pressure.

- Place patient’s far arm out straight from shoulder.

- Fold near arm across chest and flex near leg at knee.

- Maintain neutral alignment of spine and support head utilising assistant where possible.

- Grasp patient at hip and shoulder and rotate away.

- Draw upper leg towards patient’s head and flex knee at right angles to support patient in lateral position.

- Flex far arm and bring patient’s hand under their head to assist in airway support.

- Place upper arm flexed to side of patient’s head clear of the face.

- Check patient’s position is stable.

- Continuously monitor patient.

#### Specific Notes

- Exacerbation of spinal injury if alignment is not maintained. Airway takes precedence over spinal injury.
